meaning: 伏尔塔瓦河[捷克和斯洛伐克西部]
examples: The capital and largest city of Czechoslovakia, in the western part of the country on the Vltava River.
Prague: the capital and largest city of Czechoslovakia, in the western part of the country on the Vltava River.
Situated on the Banks of the vltava river, the town was built around a 13th - century castle with gothic, Renaissance and baroque elements.
The regional press is controlled almost without exception by the German publishing company Passauer Neue Presse through its subsidiary the Vltava-Labe-Press.
Even with the economic growth throughout Eastern Europe and the Balkans, 1 million pounds can still get you a central Prague penthouse overlooking the Vltava River.
